Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input A detailed description of a property management companys new rental property onboarding process Goal Generate a list of targeted open ended questions to clarify and improve understanding of the process Focus Areas Missing details decision criteria roles and responsibilities timing and sequencing exception handling Constraints Do NOT ask for SQL queries or implementation details Focus purely on conceptual understanding Questions should be open ended

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deconstruct the Process Description Identify Gaps Ambiguities

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 1 Initial Contact Documentation Review Who exactly is the property coordinator Role clarity How long does this review take Timing What happens if documents are outdated Is there a deadline for the landlord to provide them Sequencing Exceptions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 2 Property Details Entry Are there mandatory fields What if amenities or price ranges are unknown Exceptions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 3 Property Manager Assignment How is the assignment actually made Is it manual algorithmic or a mix Decision criteria What if no manager meets the specific criteria or has capacity Exceptions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 4 Introductory Meeting How is this scheduled Process detail What if the landlord and manager disagree on terms eg pets maintenance Exceptions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 5 Photography Inspection How are the photographer and inspector scheduled Timing Sequencing If repairs are needed who approves the cost and timeline Roles Decision criteria What if the landlord refuses to make necessary repairs Exceptions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 6 Marketing Pricing How does the digital marketing team get the info Handoff How often do pricing models override the landlords expected price range Decision criteria

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 7 Activation Tenant Screening What are the exact criteria for rejecting a tenant Decision criteria fair housing is mentioned later but what are the business rules Who makes the final call on choosing a tenant if multiple apply Roles

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 8 Lease Finalization Who generates the lease Roles How are custom clauses requested by the landlord handled Exceptions mentioned broadly at the end but needs specific unpacking here

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 9 Quality Checks Audits When exactly do these happen Are they bottlenecks Timing Sequencing What happens if the compliance officer or financial analyst rejects the lease price Exceptions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: General Cross cutting Communication tracking CRM PM tools is there a single source of truth End to end timeline What is the average SLA for this whole process Special insurance How does that get triggered and integrated

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Draft Questions Iterative refinement for openness and targeting

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Category 1 Roles Responsibilities Draft Who is the property coordinator Refinement How does the responsibility for a property transition from the property coordinator to the dedicated property manager and how do they ensure no details are lost in that handoff Draft Who makes the final decision on tenant selection Refinement When multiple tenants are interested who ultimately decides which applicant is selected and how are conflicts of interest or subjective preferences managed

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Category 2 Decision Criteria Draft How do you assign a property manager Refinement You mentioned assigning a property manager based on workload geography and experience Could you walk me through how these factors are weighed against each other when multiple managers are available or when none perfectly fit the profile Draft What if the landlords price is too high Refinement If the internal pricing models generated by the marketing team differ significantly from the landlords expected rental price how is that discrepancy resolved

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Category 3 Timing Sequencing Draft How long does the process take Refinement What is the typical timeline from initial landlord contact to the property being activated on the rental portal and where do the most significant delays or bottlenecks usually occur Draft Do the quality checks happen at the end Refinement You mentioned compliance financial and IT audits at the end Are these checks strictly sequential or can they happen in parallel and can they delay the tenants move in date

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Category 4 Exceptions Edge Cases Draft What if the landlord wont fix things Refinement If a property fails the safety inspection and the landlord refuses or is unable to make the necessary repairs what is the standard procedure Does the onboarding process pause or is it terminated Draft How do you handle custom leases Refinement You noted that some landlords insist on custom lease clauses or require special insurance How does the compliance team evaluate these custom clauses and at what point in the process are they negotiated Draft What if documents are missing Refinement When a property coordinator identifies missing or outdated documentation is there a specific timeframe or threshold for the landlord to provide updates before the onboarding is canceled

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Organize Group the questions logically so the interviewee can follow the flow eg Early Stages Manager Assignment Prep Tenant Finding Audits Exceptions Check against constraints Are they open ended Yes Do they ask for SQL or implementation No Do they cover the requested areas Yes missing details decision criteria roles timing exceptions

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Formatting for readability Use clear headings Add a brief introductory framing to set the tone Ensure the tone is inquisitive and professional Self correction make sure the questions arent too leading allowing the interviewee to explain their specific reality
